scenarios.title: ML Experiment Tracking and MLOps Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks about MLOps pipelines, production ML or AI workflows, pipeline execution, artifacts, stacks, deployments, experiment/model operations, observability, or ZenML package usage.
scenarios.role: The zenml skill routes ZenML-specific pipeline authoring, CLI/Client, stack integration, deployment, server, and MLOps workflow tasks.
scenarios.read_when: The task names ZenML, zenml CLI, @pipeline, @step, Client(), stacks, artifacts, materializers, orchestrators, deployers, model deployers, pipeline deployments, triggers, resource pools, schedules, DeploymentSettings, DockerSettings, or MLOps pipeline workflows.
scenarios.best_for: Writing and debugging ZenML pipelines, configuring stack components, using the CLI/Client, adapting model or agent deployment examples, troubleshooting optional dependencies, and understanding ZenML server/store layers.
scenarios.avoid_when: Avoid for generic MLflow/W&B tracking, generic FastAPI apps, generic Docker deployment, or non-ZenML orchestration tasks unless ZenML APIs or repository code are involved.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose zenml when the workflow depends on ZenML package APIs, CLI commands, stack components, deployments, server/store concepts, or repository conventions rather than only general MLOps ideas.

scenarios.title: Python Repository Maintenance
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that edit, review, test, document, package, or maintain the ZenML Python repository checkout.
scenarios.role: The zenml skill captures repository-specific maintenance rules, AGENTS guidance, optional dependency boundaries, targeted tests, docs, migrations, and PR-readiness checks for ZenML.
scenarios.read_when: The task involves modifying ZenML source, tests, docs, scripts, integrations, CLI, FastAPI server, SQLModel schemas, Alembic migrations, pyproject dependencies, AGENTS instructions, or CI-equivalent validation commands.
scenarios.best_for: Choosing targeted pytest/lint/docs/migration checks, preserving optional imports, updating CLI/client/model/schema/store layers consistently, and following ZenML contributor rules.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user only wants to use ZenML as a library without modifying its repository; use the MLOps workflow scenario entry points instead.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose zenml for repository-maintenance tasks when ZenML-specific AGENTS rules, source layout, tests, docs, migrations, or optional dependency boundaries matter.

scenarios.title: Agent Frameworks, Tooling, and Sandboxed LLM Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks about ZenML's agent examples, durable AI workflow orchestration, agent framework integrations, MCP-adjacent operational patterns, or production agent deployments built with ZenML pipelines.
scenarios.role: The zenml skill explains how to adapt ZenML agent and LLM examples into credential-aware, deployment-ready pipeline workflows.
scenarios.read_when: The task mentions ZenML with agents, LLMOps, agent_comparison, deploying_agent, LangGraph/LangChain/LlamaIndex/OpenAI agents inside ZenML, deterministic fallbacks, DeploymentSettings, or agent service deployment.
scenarios.best_for: Adapting ZenML agent examples, separating provider credentials from smoke tests, configuring pipeline deployments for agents, and routing framework matrix limitations safely.
scenarios.avoid_when: Avoid for generic agent framework tasks that do not use ZenML pipelines, deployments, or repository examples; choose the framework-specific repo skill instead when available.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose zenml for agent tasks when ZenML is the orchestration/deployment layer or when adapting ZenML's agent examples; otherwise choose the specific agent framework skill.
